title: Frank's Pretty Woman

jokes.text: We're adopting a dog-fighting pit bull. We're gonna keep it in the bar, all right?
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about adopting a dog from an animal shelter
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals they want a violent fighting dog for their bar
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven absurdity that establishes the Gang's terrible logic immediately

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're adopting a dog-fighting pit bull. We're gonna keep it in the bar, all right?

jokes.text: Because last time we tried to get a junkyard dog, we wound up getting a junkyard cat
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why they shouldn't consult someone about the dog decision
jokes.punchline: Reveals their past incompetence led to adopting a 'junkyard cat'
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and the absurd concept of a 'junkyard cat' is inherently funny

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Because last time we tried to get a junkyard dog, we wound up getting a junkyard cat

jokes.text: We don't want it to attack other dogs. Now we would like for it to attack people.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining they want to 'rehabilitate' the dog
jokes.punchline: Reveals rehabilitation means training it to attack humans instead of dogs
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with dark twist that perfectly captures the Gang's warped priorities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We don't want it to attack other dogs. Now we would like for it to attack people.

jokes.text: You can go, like, 'Dude, I got bit a pit bull in a bar.' In Paddy's! It was awesome!
jokes.setup: Explaining the customer experience of being attacked
jokes.punchline: Charlie's enthusiasm for customers getting mauled as a positive experience
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - finding the bright side of customers being attacked

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You can go, like, 'Dude, I got bit a pit bull in a bar.' In Paddy's! It was awesome!

jokes.text: Just a dial tone now. She just hung up on me, for some reason.
jokes.setup: Mac has been describing wanting a violent dog to attack customers to the animal shelter worker
jokes.punchline: Complete obliviousness to why the worker hung up
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of complete social unawareness

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Just a dial tone now. She just hung up on me, for some reason.

jokes.text: That woman seems like a prostitute. That's because she is.
jokes.setup: Dennis observing that Frank's girlfriend seems like a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure, though the premise is somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That woman seems like a prostitute.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's because she is.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make that whore my wife.
jokes.setup: Frank admitting he's dating a prostitute
jokes.punchline: His romantic plan to marry her while still calling her a whore
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - romantic sentiment expressed in the crudest possible way

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm gonna make that whore my wife.

jokes.text: [Roxy presumably does something outrageous offscreen] What the hell...? I don't know. Are they...? Oh, no!
jokes.setup: Multiple shocked reactions to something Roxy is doing
jokes.punchline: The audience imagining what horrible thing she's doing based on their horror
jokes.explanation: Effective use of offscreen action to build the character's outrageousness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What the hell...?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Are they...?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, no!

jokes.text: That-that broad is a live wire. I'm goin' outside, 'cause this place is shit hole!
jokes.setup: Frank defending Roxy after she does something outrageous
jokes.punchline: Calls her a 'live wire' as if it's a positive trait while she insults his bar
jokes.explanation: Shows Frank's blind spot when it comes to Roxy's behavior

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That-that broad is a live wire.

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: I'm goin' outside, 'cause this place is shit hole!

jokes.text: What are you cocks lookin' at?
jokes.setup: Roxy addressing other patrons
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostility and crude language to strangers
jokes.explanation: Establishes Roxy's character effectively but relies on shock value

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: What are you cocks lookin' at?

jokes.text: But she's still plowing other dudes. In fact, Tiger Woods has been calling her lately.
jokes.setup: Frank defending his relationship with Roxy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Tiger Woods as one of her clients
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity name-drop that sets up the Tiger Woods subplot

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: But she's still plowing other dudes. In fact, Tiger Woods has been calling her lately.

jokes.text: Someone love Frank for who he is? Come on!
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Frank could find someone who loves him for himself
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate dismissal of the possibility
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual cruelty toward Frank is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Someone love Frank for who he is? Come on!

jokes.text: The other day, we were hanging out under the bridge; we found box of denim, and I'm, like, 'You know, these look like good jeans in here,' and he's, like, 'You want to split it with me, 50-50?'
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to list Frank's good qualities
jokes.punchline: His example is Frank sharing found clothes from under a bridge
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures both Charlie's bizarre lifestyle and his genuine affection for Frank

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The other day, we were hanging out under the bridge; we found box of denim, and I'm, like, 'You know, these look like good jeans in here,' and he's, like, 'You want to split it with me, 50-50?'

jokes.text: Charlie! Stop wearing clothes you find washing up under bridges. Oh, they're boiled, yeah. Yeah! We boil all our denim.
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Charlie's clothing source
jokes.punchline: Charlie and Frank's casual revelation that they boil found denim
jokes.explanation: Became an iconic Always Sunny moment - the casual way they discuss boiling denim is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie! Stop wearing clothes you find washing up under bridges.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, they're boiled, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah! We boil all our denim.

jokes.text: Mac, you look bad. Really, really bad. You look fat as shit!
jokes.setup: Dennis commenting on Mac's appearance
jokes.punchline: Brutal directness about Mac's weight gain
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity and cruelty on full display, though the joke is somewhat basic

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mac, you look bad. Really, really bad. You look fat as shit!

jokes.text: Mass. Fat. Muscle. Let's be clear: fat. It's fat.
jokes.setup: Mac insisting he's gained muscle mass
jokes.punchline: Dennis's increasingly emphatic corrections
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and economy in the rapid-fire corrections

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mass. Fat. Muscle.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Let's be clear: fat. It's fat.

jokes.text: We're becoming the Gross Crew. The Gross Crew?! The Gross Crew.
jokes.setup: Dennis diagnosing the group's problems
jokes.punchline: His horror at his own terminology repeated three times
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity creating a memorable phrase through repetition

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're becoming the Gross Crew. The Gross Crew?! The Gross Crew.

jokes.text: I went from a tiny twink to the muscle-bound freak you see before you. You're not helping my argument.
jokes.setup: Mac using himself as an example of positive change
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out Mac's example actually proves his point
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Mac unknowingly undermines his own argument

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I went from a tiny twink to the muscle-bound freak you see before you.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're not helping my argument.

jokes.text: This is a chimichanga. You are becoming a chimichanga!
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Mac for eating with his mouth full
jokes.punchline: Dennis's bizarre declaration that Mac is transforming into food
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that becomes unexpectedly quotable and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This is a chimichanga. You are becoming a chimichanga!

jokes.text: Shut up, baby dick.
jokes.setup: The gang trying to help Roxy with manners
jokes.punchline: Her immediate crude insult in response to politeness
jokes.explanation: Effectively establishes Roxy's character but relies heavily on shock value

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: Shut up, baby dick.

jokes.text: No, you're already in your second act. We're all in our second acts-- don't you see that?
jokes.setup: Dee wanting to help Roxy change for her second act
jokes.punchline: Dennis's crushing realization that they're all already in decline
jokes.explanation: Dark character insight that's both funny and genuinely depressing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, you're already in your second act. We're all in our second acts-- don't you see that?

jokes.text: your first act was-was shit, and that's why you're so desperate for things to be different. But they rarely ever do get different. If anything-- God, they get worse.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why Dee wants change
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about how life generally gets worse
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissism creating unexpectedly dark philosophical insights

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: your first act was-was shit, and that's why you're so desperate for things to be different. But they rarely ever do get different. If anything-- God, they get worse.

jokes.text: He got salsa on his shirt. What's that smell? Crack. That is crack rock. She's smoking crack!
jokes.setup: Notice Mac got salsa on his shirt
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to discovering Roxy is smoking crack
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mundane observation to shocking discovery

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He got salsa on his shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's that smell?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Crack.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That is crack rock. She's smoking crack!

jokes.text: the man is carrying around trash bags filled with Mexican foods. What's that smell? Crack. That is crack rock.
jokes.setup: Dennis noting Mac's food habits
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to discovering crack
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation from food to drugs that fits the show's chaos

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: the man is carrying around trash bags filled with Mexican foods. What's that smell?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Crack. That is crack rock.

jokes.text: I signed you up on a dating Web site. I got you a date, dude! This girl does think she's meeting a millionaire. Why'd you say that, Charlie?
jokes.setup: Charlie helping Frank with online dating
jokes.punchline: Revealing he immediately undermined his own plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie logic - good intentions with poor execution

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I signed you up on a dating Web site. I got you a date, dude! This girl does think she's meeting a millionaire.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Why'd you say that, Charlie?

jokes.text: A night with a limo driver? This is a good idea.
jokes.setup: Charlie's plan for Frank to seem like a regular worker
jokes.punchline: Frank's genuine excitement about this 'opportunity'
jokes.explanation: Frank's desperation making him excited about downgrading his apparent status

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: A night with a limo driver? This is a good idea.

jokes.text: Whoa! I burned myself on a rivet.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Frank boiling denim
jokes.punchline: Charlie injuring himself in this bizarre process
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the boiling denim setup with Charlie getting hurt doing something stupid

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Whoa! I burned myself on a rivet.

jokes.text: I don't eat lunch anymore, for instance. Yeah, and on odd days, I don't eat breakfast.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his health regimen
jokes.punchline: Revealing increasingly extreme eating restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity taken to unhealthy extremes

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't eat lunch anymore, for instance. Yeah, and on odd days, I don't eat breakfast.

jokes.text: I call them hummingbirds. And although I seem relaxed, I'm actually incredibly tense at all times.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing imperceptible exercises
jokes.punchline: His constant state of tension disguised as relaxation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd character detail that perfectly captures Dennis's neuroses

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I call them hummingbirds. And although I seem relaxed, I'm actually incredibly tense at all times.

jokes.text: And although I seem relaxed, I'm actually incredibly tense at all times.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his fitness philosophy
jokes.punchline: Revealing his constant state of internal stress
jokes.explanation: Dark insight into Dennis's psychological state disguised as fitness advice

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And although I seem relaxed, I'm actually incredibly tense at all times.

jokes.text: Stop cultivating and start harvesting.
jokes.setup: Mac claiming he's cultivating mass
jokes.punchline: Dennis's farming metaphor for losing weight
jokes.explanation: Became an iconic Always Sunny quote - perfect economy and metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Stop cultivating and start harvesting.

jokes.text: You have type 2 adult-onset diabetes.
jokes.setup: Dennis being smug about being healthier than Mac
jokes.punchline: Doctor revealing Dennis has diabetes instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal that undercuts Dennis's superiority complex

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: You have type 2 adult-onset diabetes.

jokes.text: Dude, stop saying 'diabetes!' You sound like an ass...
jokes.setup: Dennis repeatedly saying diabetes
jokes.punchline: Mac's concern about how Dennis sounds rather than his health
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's skewed priorities and social awareness

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, stop saying 'diabetes!' You sound like an ass...

jokes.text: I'm not as strong as I thought, Dennis. I've hurt my back.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to lift Dennis after claiming to be strong
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury proving his weakness
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that deflates Mac's strength claims

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm not as strong as I thought, Dennis. I've hurt my back.

jokes.text: and it's tighter than dick skin, man.
jokes.setup: Roxy trying on an expensive jacket
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison in an upscale setting
jokes.explanation: Establishes character clash with environment but relies on crude shock value

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: and it's tighter than dick skin, man.

jokes.text: Screw your dick skin jacket, man. How do you like me now, gay boy?
jokes.setup: Tension with the salesperson
jokes.punchline: Roxy's explosive response and offensive language
jokes.explanation: Effective character moment but relies heavily on offensive shock value

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: Screw your dick skin jacket, man. How do you like me now, gay boy?

jokes.text: It's Tiger Woods? Your life is way more glamorous than what I was picturing.
jokes.setup: Dee learning about Roxy's supposed celebrity client
jokes.punchline: Dee's naive admiration for what she thinks is glamour
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's gullibility and twisted perspective on success

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It's Tiger Woods? Your life is way more glamorous than what I was picturing.

jokes.text: Now, help me dig these crack rocks out of my ass.
jokes.setup: Dee being impressed by Roxy's glamorous life
jokes.punchline: Immediate crash back to sordid reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dee's romanticized view with harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: Roxy
dialogue.line: Now, help me dig these crack rocks out of my ass.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm a four year old going to my sister's wedding.
jokes.setup: Frank uncomfortable in formal wear
jokes.punchline: Specific, childish comparison
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and relatable comparison that humanizes Frank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I feel like I'm a four year old going to my sister's wedding.

jokes.text: Tarnation! You look as pretty as a peach.
jokes.setup: Charlie playing a millionaire character
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top fake accent and folksy expressions
jokes.explanation: Charlie's terrible acting creates an effective character bit

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Tarnation! You look as pretty as a peach.

jokes.text: hustlin' and a-rasslin'. made a decent penny in boiled denims and, uh, the bridge business mostly.
jokes.setup: Asked about his fortune source
jokes.punchline: References his actual homeless lifestyle as business ventures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms Charlie's weird lifestyle into fake business credentials

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: hustlin' and a-rasslin'. made a decent penny in boiled denims and, uh, the bridge business mostly.

jokes.text: I found a whole case of eggs under a bridge last week-- perfect condition. None of them missing. None of them cracked.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to make conversation about bridges
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for finding garbage eggs
jokes.explanation: Sets up the egg offer and perfectly captures Frank's homeless mindset

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I found a whole case of eggs under a bridge last week-- perfect condition. None of them missing. None of them cracked.

jokes.text: Can I offer you a nice egg in this trying time?
jokes.setup: Charlie pretending to be sick and the date going badly
jokes.punchline: Frank's completely inappropriate offer of a bridge egg
jokes.explanation: Became one of the most iconic Always Sunny quotes - perfect timing, character integration, and absurdist logic

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Can I offer you a nice egg in this trying time?

jokes.text: I swallowed, like, a million blood caplets 'cause I was gonna cough, like, you know, a handful of blood to make it real.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining why his fake illness went wrong
jokes.punchline: His dangerous and illogical method acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - good intentions with completely wrong execution

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I swallowed, like, a million blood caplets 'cause I was gonna cough, like, you know, a handful of blood to make it real.

jokes.text: And I love boiling denim and banging whores! And I don't care if anybody doesn't like that about me.
jokes.setup: Frank getting emotional about the failed date
jokes.punchline: His defiant declaration of love for his weird lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Frank's emotional breakthrough that celebrates his bizarre lifestyle choices

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: And I love boiling denim and banging whores! And I don't care if anybody doesn't like that about me.

jokes.text: I know who you are. This guy's an actor. He definitely pretended to be Donovan McNabb one time when I tried out for the Eagles.
jokes.setup: Meeting the fake Tiger Woods
jokes.punchline: Dee recognizing him from another impersonation scam
jokes.explanation: Great world-building that implies this con artist has fooled them before

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I know who you are. This guy's an actor. He definitely pretended to be Donovan McNabb one time when I tried out for the Eagles.

jokes.text: I'm busted. I am an actor. My name is Don Cheadle.
jokes.setup: Being caught impersonating Tiger Woods
jokes.punchline: Claims to be a different famous person entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the lie even more ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Fake Tiger Woods
dialogue.line: I'm busted. I am an actor. My name is Don Cheadle.

jokes.text: Chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga.
jokes.setup: Mac eating chimichangas
jokes.punchline: Repetitive chanting of the word
jokes.explanation: Callback that shows Mac's complete surrender to his food addiction

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga, chimichanga.

jokes.text: What I really want? Crack. Oh. I thought you were gonna say pizza or buffalo wings or something.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking what Mac really wants
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate jump to hard drugs surprises even Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that escalates beyond even Dennis's expectations

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What I really want?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Crack.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh. I thought you were gonna say pizza or buffalo wings or something.

jokes.text: I'm still gonna pay you, but I want you to stop banging other guys. What do you say? Want to be my wife or what?
jokes.setup: Frank's romantic proposal
jokes.punchline: Treating marriage like a business transaction while still paying her
jokes.explanation: Frank's warped romanticism perfectly captured in transactional terms

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm still gonna pay you, but I want you to stop banging other guys. What do you say? Want to be my wife or what?

jokes.text: The bitch is dead! How could she be dead? I'm telling you her heart popped.
jokes.setup: Roxy collapsing during the proposal
jokes.punchline: Frank's medical diagnosis and casual announcement
jokes.explanation: Dark turn handled with characteristic Always Sunny callousness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The bitch is dead! How could she be dead? I'm telling you her heart popped.

jokes.text: Dead hooker in my apartment? That doesn't look good for me. I'm covered in blood.
jokes.setup: Group trying to figure out what to do about Roxy's death
jokes.punchline: Dee's self-centered concern about how it affects her
jokes.explanation: Shows the Gang's selfish nature even in a crisis situation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dead hooker in my apartment? That doesn't look good for me. I'm covered in blood.

jokes.text: We could just put her out in the hall. We call in an anonymous tip.
jokes.setup: Gang deciding what to do with dead body
jokes.punchline: Treating corpse disposal like a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about covering up death. Dennis's practical approach to body disposal is characteristically sociopathic.

dialogue.line: We could just put her out in the hall. We call in an anonymous tip.

jokes.text: I think Roxy would have wanted it that way. Well, there you go. Frank would know.
jokes.setup: Plan to dump Roxy's body in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Frank speaking for the dead prostitute, Dennis agreeing he's the expert
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of treating body disposal as if it's respecting the deceased's wishes

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I think Roxy would have wanted it that way.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, there you go.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank would know.

jokes.text: Roxy, God bless you. You were a good whore. You serviced me like no other whore ever did. Not only my crank, but my heart.
jokes.setup: Frank giving a eulogy for Roxy
jokes.punchline: Crude but oddly touching tribute that treats prostitution as noble work
jokes.explanation: Frank's genuine emotion expressed in characteristically crude terms creates unexpected poignancy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Roxy, God bless you. You were a good whore. You serviced me like no other whore ever did. Not only my crank, but my heart.

tmdb_overview: With Frank intent on marrying a prostitute, The Gang tries to make the best of it by attempting an image makeover to uncover the heart of gold that exists deep inside Frank’s “pretty woman.”
